Now that we've made our submission zap, we only have one thing left to do, and that's get in our appointment time onto this sheet. So in order to do that, we're going to need to make a new zap. So we're going to go ahead and create a zap by clicking on the make a zap button. And this time we're going to hook up our zap to Acuity and our Google sheet. So I'm going to go ahead and name this zap Acuity appointment time. My trigger event is going to be whenever Acuity gets a new appointment. So I'm going to go ahead and click on Acuity scheduling. If you need to type it in in the search bar, please do so. And then my trigger event is going to be new appointment. From here, I'll hit the continue button, and it'll ask you to log in and verify that Zapier can use your Acuity. I have already done so, so I'm going to go ahead and sync my account up and hit the continue button. From here, you'll choose whichever calendar you set up that your appointments are on, and then the appointment type that you want it to be looking for. So for me, that's consultation. Next, you'll hit the continue button again, and we're going to find a test appointment. And now the next thing we're going to do is set up a delay. So for our delay, our action event is going to be delay four, hit continue, and two minutes. So our value will be two, our unit will be minutes, and then we're going to hit continue. We're going to skip the test so that we don't just sit here for two minutes, and we're going to move on to our next step, which is with Google Sheets. So I'm going to choose Google Sheets here for my third step, and my action event is going to be look up spreadsheet row. So the reason that we needed that delay is so that our other Zap has time to create that spreadsheet row. So now that we're going to look it up, we're going to hit the continue button, select our Google Sheets account, and hit continue. And now we're going to tell it what sheet we want it to be looking up on. So the spreadsheet is going to be our leads and stats. The tab we want it to add to, or worksheet, is going to be incoming leads. And then the lookup column, we're going to choose email, because that's a little bit more of a unique identifier than a first or last name. And then the lookup value, we just have to match that up with the value that Acuity received from that click funnel. So there's our email. And then from here, we can hit the continue button. We're going to go ahead and have Zapier look that up. So we're going to click fetch and continue. And from here, now that it's fetched and looked up that row, we still needed to update it. So for our fourth step, we're going to choose Google Sheets again. And this time, we're going to update the spreadsheet row. And we'll hit continue. We'll choose our account, hit continue. And tell it where it's updating. So the spreadsheet is going to be our leads and stats. So worksheet will be incoming leads. And then we have to tell it how to find the place to update. So we're going to use a custom value. And our custom value for our row ID is going to come from our step above, where it looked the row ID up. So we'll add the ID right here. And now we're just going to add in the things that we needed to update. So the only thing that we need to add in is that appointment time. So we're going to go ahead and scroll down, find the date of the appointment, hit the space bar, and then the time of the appointment. And we'll go ahead and hit continue. And we'll send our test to make sure that our zap is working. It's got a green check mark, so that's good. And there's our appointment time. So now we have both of our zaps set up. So we just need to make sure that we do turn them on. So I'm going to turn this on. And now we're done with Zapier. So I'll see you in the next video.
